title: Silver( I)- Gelatin Complex : A Novel Reagent for Oxidative Phenol Coupling
author: TARASANKAR PAL
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Indian Institute of Technology, Kharagpur-721 302 
 Manuscript  received 22 December 1988, accepted 21 February 1989  

 Silver(I)- gelatin complex, a mild, efficient and new reagent has been developed for phenol coupling. When applied to  \({p}\) - cresol naphth-2-ol and emodin (1) separately, it afforded Pummerer's ketone (2) and  o,o -coupled dimer (3) for first compound, for naphth-2-ol we were left only with 1,1'-binaphth-1-ol (4) and emodin led to isoskyrln (5a) as the sole product. The method is highly selective and gifted with least polymerisation.
steps_raw:
Department of Chemistry, Indian Institute of Technology, Kharagpur-721 302 
 Manuscript  received 22 December 1988, accepted 21 February 1989  

 Silver(I)- gelatin complex, a mild, efficient and new reagent has been developed for phenol coupling. When applied to  \({p}\) - cresol naphth-2-ol and emodin (1) separately, it afforded Pummerer's ketone (2) and  o,o -coupled dimer (3) for first compound, for naphth-2-ol we were left only with 1,1'-binaphth-1-ol (4) and emodin led to isoskyrln (5a) as the sole product. The method is highly selective and gifted with least polymerisation.
